annotation_target.structure: Middle descent antecedent — bass deep, RH whispered chords
key_moments: m27 RH chords barely above silence
teaching_goals: the piece is deep in its most austere section
RH chords should float — barely touching the keys
style_tips: ppp — the quietest the piece gets
the silence between notes is music too

teaching_notes.note: The RH chords here are whispered — barely audible but still present.

target_trace.objective: Read mm. 25–28 of Prelude in E Minor, Op. 28 No. 4, view the piano roll, analyze the phrase, play in a loop, and predict the continuation.

session.content: Show me measures 25–28 of the Prelude — how deep is the harmonic descent now?
Let me view the piano roll for mm. 25–28.
Measures 25–28 are deep in the descent — the chromatic bass has moved through many semitone steps from the opening. The RH chords are now quiet, almost whispered. Let me loop mm. 25–28.
Chopin Prelude E minor mm. 25–28: deep descent, bass far from opening E, RH whispered chords.
